structData.title: Campaign delivery eligibility checklist — why impressions stop
structData.content:
If your campaign status shows ACTIVE but impressions are zero, or the campaign has suddenly stopped delivering, check these fields in order using getCampaignStatus:

1. STATUS FIELD
   The status field must be ACTIVE. If it shows PAUSED, the campaign will not serve any impressions regardless of budget or bid. Campaigns are automatically paused when daily budget is exhausted. They must be manually resumed each day or the budget must be increased. If status is ENDED, the flight end date has passed — update the flight dates.

2. BUDGET REMAINING
   The budgetRemaining field must be greater than $0.00. If it shows $0.00, the daily budget has been fully spent and delivery stops until midnight UTC reset. This is the most common cause of delivery stopping mid-day. Fix: increase dailyBudget or change pacingMode from ASAP to EVEN.

3. BID VS FLOOR PRICE
   The currentBid must be greater than or equal to bidFloor. If currentBid is below bidFloor, your DSP will submit bids that the SSP rejects — resulting in zero wins and zero impressions even if budget is available. Example: currentBid=$0.95, bidFloor=$1.20 means every auction is lost. Fix: raise the line item CPM bid to at least bidFloor + $0.10 buffer.

4. AUDIENCE SIZE
   If audienceSize shows 0, the campaign has no eligible users to target. This means the audience segment has not been populated or the segment upload failed. Check getSegmentUploadLog for hash format errors. Minimum viable audience size is 1000 matched users.

5. FLIGHT DATES
   The flightEnd date must be in the future. If today's date is past flightEnd, the campaign has expired and will not serve. Update the end date to extend the flight.

6. CREATIVE APPROVAL
   At least one creative attached to the line item must have reviewStatus=APPROVED. A creative in DISAPPROVED or PENDING state cannot serve. Check getCreativeStatus for rejection reason codes.
